summary: Do not ship as-is: the connector step can be redirected to an arbitrary script with borrowed auth, run records persist raw step outputs/errors without secret redaction, and the auth-env route will hand out connector auth to any reachable caller without an in-route locality check.

findings.title: `connector` steps can override `scriptPath` and execute arbitrary local code with borrowed connector secrets/tokens
findings.recommendation: Remove the YAML-controlled `scriptPath` override for production runs, or strictly resolve and verify the executable against the installed connector package for `resolved.connector` before injecting `authEnv`. As written, an automation can name a real connector to obtain scoped auth from `/auth-env` and then run any local Node script with that secret-bearing environment.

findings.title: Run records and emitted step results persist raw command/API/connector output with no secret-value redaction
findings.recommendation: Redact current vault secret/token values before emitting or persisting `step_output_chunk`, `step.result`, and `error` fields, or avoid storing raw bodies/stdout/stderr entirely. Today `local_command` stores full stdout/stderr, `api_call` stores response bodies, and `connector` stores returned payloads/errors verbatim in `record.steps[*]`, so any echoed bearer/token/secret is written to disk.

findings.title: `auth-env` has no caller-locality/authz check before returning connector secrets or fresh OAuth access tokens
findings.recommendation: Gate this route to trusted local callers only, e.g. verify loopback source plus an unguessable server-to-server credential or equivalent same-host capability. Right now any process that can reach the Next server and knows a connector id can POST here and receive that connector's scoped secrets or a fresh access token.

findings.title: `auth-env` will return scoped secrets even for connectors declared with `auth: "none"`
findings.recommendation: Make `auth: "none"` return an empty env, and only materialize `secret_scope` secrets for `auth: "api_key"`. The current `api_key (or none)` branch over-delivers relative to the documented contract and makes a future misconfigured connector able to borrow secrets it should not receive.
